pattern: Precomputing each suffix's distinct-letter count once lets a single left-to-right sweep compare it against a running left distinct count to find every good split.
hints: Precompute, for every possible split point, how many distinct letters appear on the right side, by scanning once from the end (or by shrinking a full frequency table as you sweep forward).
Then walk left to right maintaining the distinct letter count of the left side seen so far.
A split right after position i is good exactly when the left side's distinct count through i equals the right side's distinct count starting at i + 1.

edge_inputs.description: two distinct characters, one good split
single character has no valid split
one rare letter surrounded by a repeated letter
distinct run followed by its own repeat
long identical run then a distinct tail

stress.description: long string over a small alphabet so distinct counts collide often
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    alphabet = 'abcde'
    return [''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))]
